# Competition Rules & Entry Process

<callout icon="🏆" color="blue_bg">
	**$1,000 IN CREDITS | 4 WINNERS | ANNOUNCED LIVE AT THE SUMMIT, WED JUL 8, 2026** | Be present to win. All times SGT.
</callout>

<callout icon="🧭" color="gray_bg">
	**Quick orientation:** *joining* the challenge means registering (free, see the Campaign page). *Entering* the competition means graduating, your Day 14 submission is automatically your entry. This doc is about the competition.
</callout>

## How to Enter (read this twice)

<callout icon="✅" color="green_bg">
	**There is no separate entry form to chase.** If you complete the challenge and graduate, your Day 14 final submission IS your competition entry.
	To be eligible: complete the 6 graded submissions (Days 3, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14), pass both gates (Day 8 video and Day 14 final), and attend the Summit on Wed Jul 8.
</callout>

## What You Submit on Day 14, and Exactly How

<callout icon="⛔" color="red_bg">
	**This is the part that confused people last time.** A separate box for each item. Do not paste everything into one.
</callout>

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg">
<td>#</td>
<td>Field</td>
<td>What goes in it</td>
<td>File or link</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>1</td>
<td>Your agent</td>
<td>A link to your Claude Project, or a 60 to 90 sec screen recording</td>
<td>Link or file</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>2</td>
<td>Your one-page brief</td>
<td>PDF or doc</td>
<td>File or link</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>3</td>
<td>Demo idea</td>
<td>One content idea and ready-to-run prompt your agent produced</td>
<td>Text or link</td>
</tr>
</table>

Printed on the form: "Paste a link or upload a file in each box. Video links welcome."

<callout icon="🧪" color="orange_bg">
	**Mandatory pre-launch form test.** Before the rules go live, a team member submits a full dummy entry (link, file, text) and confirms all three land in the entries Drive (Matt and Danny added). This is the single fix for last challenge's failure.
</callout>

## Prizes: $1,000 in Nas.com Credits

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg">
<td>Prize</td>
<td>Amount</td>
<td>For</td>
</tr>
<tr><td>Grand Prize</td><td>$500 in credits</td><td>Best overall agent, brief, and output quality</td></tr>
<tr><td>Runner-Up</td><td>$300 in credits</td><td>Second best overall</td></tr>
<tr><td>Honorable Mention</td><td>$100 in credits</td><td>Third, a strong agent worth recognizing</td></tr>
<tr><td>Community MVP</td><td>$100 in credits</td><td>Most helpful member during the 15 days (community-voted)</td></tr>
</table>

Nas.com credits, same as last challenge. Issued via Nas.com after the Summit (about Jul 9). $1,000 across 4 prizes, matching the previous challenge exactly. The Community MVP is voted by members (a poll posted after graduation), not by the judges.

## Judging Timeline (all SGT)

1. Sat Jul 4 (Day 14), 11:59pm: final submissions close.
2. Sun Jul 5 (Graduation): host team reviews every entry against the rubric.
3. Sun Jul 5 evening: finalists shortlisted. The community vote for the Community MVP opens (poll posted in the community).
4. Mon Jul 6 to Tue Jul 7: winners chosen (verified as challenge graduates).
5. Wed Jul 8 (Summit): Jay verifies each winner is a challenge graduate, then winners announced live. You must be present.
6. Wed Jul 8 to Jul 9: credits issued.

## Rules

- Judges: Danny, Matt, host team. Decisions final.
- You must be present at the Summit when winners are announced.
- The rubric is published on Day 1.
- Each winner is verified as a challenge graduate (by Jay) before the slide.
- AI-assisted is the point, but the business, customer choice, voice, and setup must be yours. One entry per person.

---
*Reconcile field-for-field with the live submission form before launch. AI SCHOOL. NAS.com. CONFIDENTIAL.*
